---
title: "image-acquisition"
format: html
---

Obtaining the best image implies the three following conditions:

- adequate patient position
- adequate probe position
- adequate ultrasound device settings

When starting the ultrasound training journey, most of the trainees may get frustrated as they are slow for obtaining the good view. It is always important to keep in mind that becoming proficient in ultrasound requires building a good spatial coordination, sometimes called "target-hand-eye" coordination. And the only way to obtain this is to practice consistently. The aim of the training program is therefore not only to provide information but also to accompany the trainees during their first exams, so that they are able to improve themselves over time by practicing during their shifts.
